taxonomy: × Agrositanion saxicola is a rare and unique plant species, and it belongs to the family of flowering plants, which is broadly classified under the kingdom Plantae. Further classification places it in the order Lamiales, which includes a diverse group of plants such as mint, basil, and lavender. The family to which × Agrositanion saxicola belongs is Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. At the level of classification below the family, it is placed in the subfamily Nepetoideae, which includes plants like oregano and rosemary. The complete classification of × Agrositanion saxicola is therefore: Kingdom: Plantae, Order: Lamiales, Family: Lamiaceae, Subfamily: Nepetoideae.
